heading: Sense of Congress on enforcement of multilateral sanctions regime and expansion and implementation of sanctions laws

children.text: prompt enforcement of the current multilateral sanctions regime with respect to Iran;

children.label: A
children.text: intensified monitoring by the President and the designees of the President, including the Secretary of the Treasury, the Secretary of State, and senior officials in the intelligence community (as defined in ), as appropriate;section 401a(4) of title 50

children.label: B
children.text: more extensive use of extraordinary authorities provided for under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.) and other sanctions laws;50 U.S.C. 1701

children.label: C
children.text: reallocation of resources to provide the personnel necessary, within the Department of the Treasury, the Department of State, and the Department of Commerce, and, where appropriate, the intelligence community, to apply and enforce sanctions; and

children.label: D
children.text: expanded cooperation with international sanctions enforcement efforts;

children.label: A
children.text: the provision of energy-related services to Iran;

children.label: B
children.text: the provision of insurance and reinsurance services to Iran;

children.label: C
children.text: the provision of shipping services to Iran; and

children.label: D
children.text: those Iranian financial institutions not yet designated for the imposition of sanctions that may be acting as intermediaries for Iranian financial institutions that are designated for the imposition of sanctions; and

children.label: A
children.text: the activities of telecommunications, Internet, and satellite service providers, in and outside of Iran, to ensure that such providers are not participating in or facilitating, directly or indirectly, the evasion of the sanctions regime with respect to Iran or violations of the human rights of the people of Iran;

children.label: B
children.text: the activities of financial institutions or other businesses or government agencies, in or outside of Iran, not yet designated for the imposition of sanctions; and

children.label: C
children.text: urgent and ongoing evaluation of Iran’s energy, national security, financial, and telecommunications sectors, to gauge the effects of, and possible defects in, particular sanctions, with prompt efforts to correct any gaps in the existing sanctions regime with respect to Iran.
